author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
gurudeva!
kṛpā⋅bindu diyā, kara ei dāse,
tṛṇāpekṣā ati hīna
sakala⋅sahane, bala diyā kara,
nija⋅māne spṛhā-hīna

sakale sammāna, karite śakati,
deha nātha! jathāyatha
tabe ta’ gāiba, harināma⋅sukhe,
aparādha ha’be hata

kabe hena kṛpā, labhiyā e jana,
kṛtārtha hoibe nātha!
śakti⋅buddhi⋅hīna, āmi ati dīna,
kara more ātma⋅sātha

jogyatā⋅vicāre, kichu nāhi pāi,
tomāra karuṇā sāra
karuṇā nā haile, kā̐diyā kā̐diyā,
prāṇa nā rākhiba āra
translation_intro: From The Harmonist, June 1930. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
en_translation:
Gurudeva ! By administering a particle of thy mercy, Make this servant of Thine Infinitely more humble than a blade of grass. Imparting strength To bear all trials Make me unsolicitous of my own honour.

Give me, my Lord, The strength to honour all In the manner that is due. It is only then that with joy I will sing the Name of Hari, And all offence will end.

When will this person, Gaining such mercy, Be blessed, indeed, O Lord ? Destitute of strength and wit, I am very low; May thou make me thine.

To judge of my fitness, I find T have none, Thy mercy is essential. If thou art not merciful, I will not cease weeping Till life itself is spent out.
